title: Nauclea sessilifructus
taxonomy: Nauclea sessilifructus belongs to the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants. It is native to tropical regions of Africa and Asia. This plant is classified as a shrub or a small tree, with a height range of 3-10 meters. Nauclea sessilifructus is also known as 'African Nauclea' or 'Sida tree'. The classification of Nauclea sessilifructus is further detailed at the genus and species levels, but overall, it falls under the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Nauclea sessilifructus, a plant native to Southeast Asia, has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits. Its bark and roots are used in traditional remedies to treat fever, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal issues. In some cultures, it is also used to treat snakebites and other venomous bites due to its antivenom properties. Additionally, the plant has been found to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential health benefits of Nauclea sessilifructus.

side_effects: Nauclea sessilifructus is reported to cause gastrointestinal side effects such as nausea and vomiting due to its strong purgative properties. The excessive use of Nauclea sessilifructus can lead to electrolyte imbalance and dehydration. Additionally, its consumption is associated with cardiovascular issues and irregular heartbeat in some individuals.
